the job is fetched wrongly in this position.
pply at the original posting: https://epilot.recruitee.com/o/senior-product-engineer-fullstack
JOB DESCRIPTION

are you ready to be a technology leader in the saas space? join epilot! we are looking for product minded engineers not only coders. we're seeking builders who bridge technical expertise with product strategy. if you're passionate about impactful software, apply now! (learn more about our understanding of being a product engineer here ) epilot is building a saas product to sell complex products online, focusing on solving ecommerce in the rapidly transforming energy market. our mission: make selling complex energy products as easy as selling a pair of shoes online. as software engineer at epilot you will be a driving force in shaping this mission. what makes working as a software engineer at epilot so special? our unique engineering culture is defined by a few core engineering principles. we "epilots" are a team of experts from the fields of software development, energy management, product management and sales. in order to bring our solution even faster to the top in the energy world, we are looking for you as a senior product engineer (full stack) .

</user_query>

The description looks truncated and poorly formatted — I'll trace how Recruitee postings are fetched and parsed for this position.

input.command:
cd /Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume && python3 -c "
import json, requests
from relocation_jobs.core.ats_detection import HEADERS

# List offers
r = requests.get('https://epilot.recruitee.com/api/offers/', headers=HEADERS, timeout=10)
offers = r.json().get('offers', [])
slug = 'senior-product-engineer-fullstack'
offer = next(o for o in offers if o.get('slug') == slug)
print('Offer ID:', offer['id'])
print('List keys:', sorted(offer.keys()))
print()

# Detail
d = requests.get(f\"https://epilot.recruitee.com/api/offers/{offer['id']}\", headers=HEADERS, timeout=10)
detail = d.json().get('offer', {})
print('Detail keys:', sorted(detail.keys()))
print()

# Show all text-ish fields
for k, v in sorted(detail.items()):
    if isinstance(v, str) and len(v) > 20:
        print(f'=== {k} ({len(v)} chars) ===')
        print(v[:300])
        print('...')
        print()
    elif isinstance(v, list) and v:
        print(f'=== {k} (list len={len(v)}) ===')
        print(json.dumps(v[:2], indent=2)[:500])
        print()
"
input.description: Inspect full Recruitee API offer payload fields

content.text:
Recruitee splits content into `description` and `requirements`; we're only reading `description`. Checking whether that's consistent and how the UI lowercases text.

[REDACTED]

cd /Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume && python3 -c "
import requests
from relocation_jobs.core.ats_detection import HEADERS
from relocation_jobs.scrape.descriptions import html_to_readable

r = requests.get('https://epilot.recruitee.com/api/offers/1486625', headers=HEADERS, timeout=10)
offer = r.json()['offer']
desc = offer.get('description', '')
req = offer.get('requirements', '')
combined = html_to_readable(desc + '\n\n' + req)
print(f'desc html: {len(desc)}, req html: {len(req)}, combined text: {len(combined)}')
print('--- first 400 ---')
print(combined[:400])
print('--- sample middle ---')
print(combined[1000:1400])
print('--- last 400 ---')
print(combined[-400:])
"

content.text:
Recruitee stores the intro in `description` and the main body in `requirements`; we only read `description`. Updating the fetcher to combine both.

[REDACTED]

assert "Ship features end to end" in text


def test_needs_recruitee_refetch():
    intro_only = (
        "Are you ready to be a technology leader in the SaaS space? Join epilot!\n\n"
        "We are looking for product minded engineers not only coders."
    )
    full = intro_only + "\n\nWhat awaits you\n\n• Ship features end to end"
    assert needs_recruitee_refetch("") is True
    assert needs_recruitee_refetch(intro_only) is True
    assert needs_recruitee_refetch(full) is False

input.new_string:
from relocation_jobs.scrape.job_text import fetch_recruitee_job_text
from relocation_jobs.scrape.descriptions import needs_recruitee_refetch

input.command: cd /Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume && python3 scripts/fix_job_descriptions.py --country germany --ats recruitee --dry-run 2>&1 | head -40
input.description: Dry-run recruitee refetch for Germany jobs

content.text:
**Root cause:** Recruitee splits postings into two API fields — `description` (short intro) and `requirements` (the main body: responsibilities, qualifications, etc.). We only read `description`, so for epilot you got ~1,068 chars ending at “Senior Product Engineer (Full Stack)” instead of the full ~5,651-char posting.

**Fix:** `fetch_recruitee_job_text` now combines both fields before converting to readable text.

**To refresh this position:** Re-fetch the description from the company panel (Fetch JD button), or run:

```bash
python3 scripts/fix_job_descriptions.py --country germany --ats recruitee
```

That script now flags truncated Recruitee descriptions (short intro-only text with no bullet list) for refetch. Future scrapes and enrich runs will pick up the full text automatically.

[REDACTED]

Running the Recruitee refetch script for Germany jobs.

[REDACTED]

| Natuvion | (Senior) SAP ABAP Developer/Architect – Product Development | 4,450 chars |
| Natuvion | (Senior) SAP ABAP Developer/Architect – Technical Migration | 4,698 chars |
| Natuvion | Senior SAP IS-U & FI/CO ABAP Application Developer | 4,666 chars |

7 other Recruitee jobs were already fine and skipped. The epilot posting should now show the full JD including “What awaits you”, tech stack, and qualifications — refresh the company page to see it.
